author: Dr. Emily Rubin (Palliative Care)
content:
PALLIATIVE CARE / FAMILY MEETING NOTE — UPMC
Date: 5/18/2027 (ICU day ~16)

Attendees: Sister Carol (surrogate decision-maker), the patient's son and daughter (recently reconciled with the patient), MICU team, palliative care.

SITUATION as presented to the family: ~2 weeks in the ICU; persistent high fevers and refractory shock now requiring two vasopressors; unresponsive; worsening organ function with a MELD ~40; an extensive infectious workup has not identified a source and she has not improved on broad-spectrum antibiotics. The team conveyed a guarded-to-poor prognosis.

FAMILY: The sister, as surrogate, and the two children raised the question of COMFORT MEASURES, wanting to avoid prolonging suffering if she will not recover. They also expressed wanting to be sure 'everything reasonable' had been considered. Emotional given the recent reconciliation. They asked for a short time to gather as a family.

This note documents the deliberation and the family's values. No change in code status or treatment has been enacted at this time; the team will reconvene with the family. (Recommendation/plan intentionally brief here pending that discussion.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Emily Rubin, MD (Palliative Care). 5/18/2027.
